codeReview.summary: Comprehensive protected routes tests fully cover all 6 VAL-PROTECT assertions (VAL-PROTECT-001 through VAL-PROTECT-006) plus additional coverage for authenticated access and redirect persistence. Tests pass consistently (12/12). Implementation correctly mirrors the actual route protection logic in ProtectedRoute.tsx and GuestRoute.tsx components.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The e2e-worker skill says 'Startup and cleanup are handled by `worker-base`', but the transcript shows the worker manually started backend and frontend services. The skill procedure may not reflect actual execution flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'service.start backend', 'service.start frontend' manually invoked. Skill says these are handled by worker-base.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says to create shared helpers in `e2e/helpers.ts`, but this file does not exist. Workers define helper functions inline in their test files. The convention is aspirational but not enforced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md: 'Create shared helpers in `e2e/helpers.ts`: registerAndGetToken(), loginAndGetToken(), etc.' - helpers.ts does not exist; worker defined helpers inline in protected-routes-comprehensive.spec.ts

summary: Feature e2e-protected-routes-comprehensive passes scrutiny. The 330-line test file implements 12 tests covering all 6 required VAL-PROTECT assertions plus additional edge cases. Tests verify: (1) /session, /history, /profile, /settings redirect unauthenticated to /login; (2) authenticated users on /login or /register redirect to home; (3) authenticated users can access protected routes; (4) redirects go to /login specifically, not /. Implementation matches actual route protection components (ProtectedRoute.tsx, GuestRoute.tsx). All 12 tests pass. Two non-blocking shared-state observations: skill says worker-base handles service startup but worker did it manually; AGENTS.md mentions helpers.ts which doesn't exist.
